title: Psychotria megalocalyx
intro: Psychotria megalocalyx is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Madagascar wild coffee. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria megalocalyx include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions in Madagascar and other parts of the world.
